summaryTemplate: {{className}} is the {{patternRole}}. Each grammar rule is a class with a virtual interpret(Context&); composite expressions interpret their children to evaluate a sentence. Pattern body lives at {{file}}:{{lineStart}}-{{lineEnd}}.
chosenRationaleTemplate: Tagged as Interpreter because {{className}} declares a virtual interpret/evaluate method (lines {{lineStart}}-{{lineEnd}}), the per-grammar-rule evaluation shape.

interpreter_interpret_method.note: The interpret operation. Each grammar rule evaluates itself against a shared context; composites recurse into their children.

runnerUpComparisonTemplates.Composite: Interpreter expression trees are usually built with Composite, but the Interpreter tag is about the interpret(Context&) evaluation contract, not the part-whole nesting itself.
